people_highlights.name: Ethan Mollick
people_highlights.post:
GPT-5.5 Pro faces its hardest academic challenge: to apply the technique from a paper analyzing which word pairs were funny & why to come up with its own

It came up with scrotum snorkel, tuba subpoena, waffle coffin, toad commode, diarrhea tiara, banana tribunal & muffin
people_highlights.why: Recent post from AI researcher at Wharton

people_highlights.name: AWS
people_highlights.post:
David Yanacek joined Amazon in 2006, forecasting how many servers they'd need for Black Friday's peak minute. 

Then EC2 launched and changed everything. Now a Sr. Principal Engineer on AWS Agentic AI, his mission hasn't changed: making developers' lives easier—because he is one.
people_highlights.why: Recent post from Official at AWS

people_highlights.name: Simon Willison
people_highlights.post: Also a great example of positive contribution to open source by wanderingmeow - you don't need to contribute code to have a positive impact, just providing detailed feedback and confirmation that something like this works is enormously useful
